title: Gear-Driven Animation Control Console
description: A mechanical control console where interlocking gears drive the animation of a box. The user manipulates gear speed, direction, and engagement to control the box's animation playback, reverse it, chain animation sequences, and fine-tune timing through a series of interactive steps with validated parameters.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a gear-driven animation control console. Load both BoxAnimated.glb and gears.glb using GLTFLoader from a CDN. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 6) looking at the origin. Add ambient light (intensity 0.5) and a directional light at (5, 8, 5) with intensity 1.2 and shadow mapping enabled. Place the gears model on the left side at (-2, 0, 0) and the BoxAnimated model on the right at (2, 0, 0). Connect them visually with a thin cylinder mesh acting as a drive shaft between them. Extract all AnimationClips from both models and create AnimationMixers. Implement these 8 interaction steps with parameter validation: (1) A speed slider (range 0.1 to 3.0, step 0.1) that controls gear rotation speed and simultaneously scales the box animation timeScale. Validate the value is within range. (2) A direction toggle button that reverses both the gear rotation direction and box animation playback direction. Track and validate state changes. (3) A gear engagement checkbox that when unchecked stops the drive shaft rotation and pauses the box animation while gears keep spinning freely. Validate engagement state. (4) A numeric input for animation blend weight (0.0 to 1.0) that crossfades between box animation clips if multiple exist. Validate float precision to one decimal. (5) A phase offset slider (-180 to 180 degrees) that offsets the gear mesh rotation relative to the animation timeline. Validate integer degree values. (6) A dropdown to select easing mode (linear, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut) applied to the box animation interpolation. Validate against allowed values. (7) A loop count input (1-10) that sets how many times the box animation loops before pausing, with gears slowing proportionally on the final loop. Validate integer range. (8) A reset button that smoothly animates all parameters back to defaults over 1 second using TWEEN or manual lerp. Validate that all parameters return to initial values. Display a status panel showing current values of all parameters. Use OrbitControls for camera interaction. Render on a dark background with a ground plane receiving shadows.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  gearRotationSpeed: number,         // current rotation speed of gears in radians per frame
  boxAnimationTimeScale: number,         // time scale multiplier for box animation playback
  isReversed: boolean,         // whether animation direction is reversed
  isEngaged: boolean,         // whether gears are engaged with drive shaft
  driveShaftRotation: number,         // current rotation angle of drive shaft in radians
  boxAnimationTime: number,         // current playback time of box animation in seconds
  chainSequenceIndex: number,         // index of current animation sequence in chain (0-based)
  timingOffset: number,         // fine-tuning offset applied to animation timing in milliseconds
  gearMeshCount: number,         // total number of gear meshes loaded from gears.glb
  isAnimationPlaying: boolean,         // whether box animation is currently playing
}
